name: weekly_plan
comment: Documentation of the weekly plan contract. The authoritative schema is the WeeklyPlan Pydantic model in backend/modules/plan_module.py (used as OpenAI structured output). Strength fields (split_type, focus, exercises, autoregulation) are optional extensions so legacy cardio plans remain valid.

split_type.description: Strength split for the week. 2-3 training days -> full_body; 4 -> upper_lower; 5-6 -> push_pull_legs. Null if no strength training.

rationale.description: A short justification explaining why this plan was generated according to the user's preferences, constraints, and recent Whoop recovery.

revision.description: A message indicating any revisions made to the plan.

start.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). Must be a Sunday.

end.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). Must be a Saturday.

workoutsByDay.description: Dictionary of workouts by weekday. Days already in the past are null. Leave at least one full rest day per week.

target_rir.description: Reps In Reserve target (1-3 typical); intensity is proximity to failure, not %1RM.

progression.description: Progressive overload rule, e.g. 'add reps to top of range on all sets, then add weight and return to bottom of range'.

type.description: Activity type; strength sessions use 'traditional strength training'. Must be a valid HealthKit-compatible type from VALID_WORKOUT_TYPES.

durationMin.description: Session length including warm-up and rest periods.

focus.description: Session focus for strength workouts; null for cardio/recovery.

exercises.description: 4-6 exercises for strength sessions, compounds first; null for cardio/recovery. Weekly volume target: ~10-20 working sets per muscle group (start 8-10 when returning from a break).

autoregulation.description: On strength sessions, 'scale_by_recovery' means day-of coaching scales the session by the morning's Whoop recovery: green = as planned, yellow = hold volume, red = cut volume or swap to light/mobility day.
